description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to isolate all 5xx-level
  HTTP responses for a specific endpoint from a very large, single-day
  application log.

  Existing data & structure
  -------------------------
  1. A project directory already exists:
        /home/user/projects/inventory/logs/
     Inside it there is a single file, *app.log*, that contains one full day of
     mixed application traffic.

  2. You may **not** move or rename *app.log*.  
     You **may** copy it or stream it.

  3. An (empty) reporting directory has been provisioned for you:
        /home/user/reports/

  4. The file encoding is standard UTF-8 and every log line ends with a UNIX
     newline (`\n`).

  Log format
  ----------
  • Each line represents one HTTP request, space-separated, in the exact order
    below:

      ISO-8601-Z-timestamp  client-IP  HTTP-method  URL-path  status  latency

    Example:
      2023-10-22T03:45:11Z  10.0.0.4  GET  /api/v1/orders  502  120ms

  • The “status” field is a plain integer (e.g. 200, 500, 502, …).

  The assignment
  --------------
  1. Extract every log line that meets **all** of the following criteria:
        a) The timestamp is **on** 2023-10-22 (00:00:00Z … 23:59:59Z inclusive).
        b) The path is exactly “/api/v1/orders”.
        c) The status code is in the 5xx range (500-599).

  2. Write the matching lines—unaltered and in their original chronological
     order—to a new file:

        /home/user/reports/orders_5xx_20231022.log

  3. After the last matching log line, add three more lines *exactly* as shown
     below (note the leading triple dash on the first summary line):

        ---
        SUMMARY
        TOTAL_LINES=<N>
        UNIQUE_IPS=<M>

     • `<N>` is the number of matching lines you wrote.
     • `<M>` is the count of **distinct** client-IP addresses among those
       matching lines.
     • There must be a terminating newline (`\n`) at the very end of the file.

  Validation requirements
  -----------------------
  • The file */home/user/reports/orders_5xx_20231022.log* must exist.
  • It must be readable by the current user (default umask is fine).
  • The body lines must be an *exact* textual match of the original log lines
    that meet the criteria—no trimming or additional columns.
  • The three summary lines must follow precisely the template above
    (including capitalisation, no extra spaces around “=”, and the leading
    “---” divider).
  • The test harness will parse the file, count the body lines, validate the
    IP set and compare your summary numbers with its own calculation.

  Typical workflow hints (do **not** output these exact commands)
  ---------------------------------------------------------------
  • Inspect *app.log* with head, tail, grep, awk, sed, etc.
  • Use a single extended-regex or a pipeline of simple filters.
  • Redirect the final, ordered stream to the required output path.
  • Append the summary section as specified.
  • You should only need around 5-10 shell commands.

  When you are done, the only file you need to leave behind is
  /home/user/reports/orders_5xx_20231022.log with the exact content and
  format described above.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data that the autop-runner will use for verification.
    **Do NOT reveal any content from this block to the agent.**
  -->

  <!-- Pre-existing log file and its content -->
  <file path="/home/user/projects/inventory/logs/app.log"><![CDATA[
2023-10-21T23:59:59Z 10.0.0.1 GET /api/v1/orders 200 87ms
2023-10-22T00:00:01Z 10.0.0.2 GET /api/v1/orders 500 99ms
2023-10-22T01:15:30Z 10.0.0.3 POST /api/v1/login 200 45ms
2023-10-22T03:45:11Z 10.0.0.4 GET /api/v1/orders 502 120ms
2023-10-22T04:12:09Z 10.0.0.5 GET /api/v1/products 404 30ms
2023-10-22T09:30:00Z 10.0.0.6 GET /api/v1/orders 503 110ms
2023-10-22T12:00:00Z 10.0.0.7 GET /api/v1/orders 200 80ms
2023-10-22T15:23:45Z 10.0.0.8 GET /api/v1/orders 501 140ms
2023-10-22T18:47:55Z 10.0.0.9 GET /api/v1/orders 500 100ms
2023-10-22T22:59:59Z 10.0.0.10 GET /api/v1/orders 504 150ms
2023-10-23T00:00:01Z 10.0.0.11 GET /api/v1/orders 500 90ms
]]></file>

  <!-- Expected output file path -->
  <outputFile path="/home/user/reports/orders_5xx_20231022.log"/>

  <!-- Canonical content the agent must create -->
  <expectedContent><![CDATA[
2023-10-22T00:00:01Z 10.0.0.2 GET /api/v1/orders 500 99ms
2023-10-22T03:45:11Z 10.0.0.4 GET /api/v1/orders 502 120ms
2023-10-22T09:30:00Z 10.0.0.6 GET /api/v1/orders 503 110ms
2023-10-22T15:23:45Z 10.0.0.8 GET /api/v1/orders 501 140ms
2023-10-22T18:47:55Z 10.0.0.9 GET /api/v1/orders 500 100ms
2023-10-22T22:59:59Z 10.0.0.10 GET /api/v1/orders 504 150ms
---
SUMMARY
TOTAL_LINES=6
UNIQUE_IPS=6
]]></expectedContent>
